3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5.

7.     <PropertyGroup>

9.     </PropertyGroup>

16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>

   </Target>
 </Project>

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

This project has **two custom targets** that break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)

**Problem:** This target has **no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes** and writes a file with `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` — a volatile value that changes every second. It will:
- Always re-run (no skip condition)
- Always produce a different file, which invalidates downstream targets like `CoreCompile`

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)

**Problem:** This target also has **no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes**, so MSBuild can never skip it. It runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes `git-hash.g.cs` on every build. Even when the git hash hasn't changed, the file is unconditionally overwritten (`Overwrite="true"`), potentially triggering recompilation due to the timestamp change on the file.

---

### Why This Matters

On a no-op build (nothing changed), both targets re-execute. `GenerateTimestamp` always produces a new file, guaranteeing `CoreCompile` sees a changed input and recompiles everything. A correct incremental build should skip all of these.

### Fixes

| Target | Fix |
|--------|-----|
| `GenerateTimestamp` | Add `Inputs`/`Outputs` or remove the volatile `DateTime::Now` value. If a timestamp is truly needed, gate it behind a hash/sentinel so the file content only changes when source files change. |
| `EmbedGitHash` | Add `Inputs="@(Compile)"` and `Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"`, and use a conditional write (only write when the hash actually changes) to avoid touching the file's modification time unnecessarily. |
